methods.name: CalculatingError(CalcErrorType, uint, string, string, string, uint, ref CalcStatus)
methods.signature: void CalculatingError(CalcErrorType errorType, uint errorCode, string context, string message, string expression, uint options, ref CalcStatus status)

params.name: status
params.doc: Calculation status, to stop calculation process return Aborted or Interrupted

methods.name: CalculatingProgress(CalcProgressEvent, uint, string, ref CalcStatus)
methods.signature: void CalculatingProgress(CalcProgressEvent progressEvent, uint progressValue, string text, ref CalcStatus status)

params.name: progressValue
params.doc: Progress value for the current event

params.name: status
params.doc: Calculation status after the event has been processed, the caller can change the state to stop the calculation process

methods.name: UndefinedVariable(string, out string)
methods.signature: void UndefinedVariable(string variableName, out string value)
methods.remarks: The undefined variable event can be used to provide a hook into the data model or API of another software system. If a variable is referred to in your calculations which doesn't exist then your event handler will be called and you have the opportunity to provide a value for the referenced name. Note that once a value has been provided the variable will then exist in Tedds so the undefined event will not fire again for that variable unless it is explicitly deleted.

params.name: value
params.doc: Value to be assigned to variable

methods.name: UndefinedVariable2(string, out string, out string)
methods.signature: void UndefinedVariable2(string variableName, out string value, out string attributes)
methods.remarks: The undefined variable event can be used to provide a hook into the data model or API of another software system. If a variable is referred to in your calculations which doesn't exist then your event handler will be called and you have the opportunity to provide a value for the referenced name. Note that once a value has been provided the variable will then exist in Tedds so the undefined event will not fire again for that variable unless it is explicitly deleted.

params.name: value
params.doc: Value to be assigned to variable

params.name: attributes
params.doc: Attributes to be assigned to variable
